heuristics.name: Use data as the medium
heuristics.description: The dataset is the core artifact, not supporting evidence.

heuristics.name: Make hidden systems visible
heuristics.description: Expose behavior that is technically public but socially illegible.

heuristics.name: Build a one-question UX
heuristics.description: The project should answer one clear question immediately.

heuristics.name: Keep it weekend-scale
heuristics.description: Favor thin prototypes, cheap hardware, static artifacts, and commodity APIs.

heuristics.name: Make the headline the product
heuristics.description: The title, first sentence, and screenshot should explain the whole artifact.

heuristics.name: Explain how it works
heuristics.description: A methodology note, caveat, or source explanation is part of the interface.

heuristics.name: Plan for ephemerality
heuristics.description: Assume unaffiliated data sources can disappear and build snapshot mode.

heuristics.name: Treat reaction as output
heuristics.description: Press, backlash, shutdowns, and remixing reveal what the project touched.

heuristics.name: Separate publicness from permission
heuristics.description: Public data can still create harm when aggregated, ranked, or reframed.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Detect platform quirks
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: A platform or public system emits predictable, overlooked, or default artifacts.
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Start by identifying the accidental data residue before proposing an interface.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Minimum viable stunt
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: An idea can demonstrate its core surprise with one collector, one store, and one public screen.
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Scope to the smallest functional artifact that proves the idea and can be explained in one sentence.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Execution as idea muscle
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: A backlog idea is small enough to build and launch quickly.
examples: public quote about following through on ideas
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Prefer action-oriented prototypes over long speculative strategy unless the user asks for planning.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Use a familiar interface to reframe strange data
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: A public dataset is hard to read but maps naturally onto an existing UI metaphor.
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Choose an interface that users already understand, then make provenance and limitations visible.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Archive ephemeral systems
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: The upstream source may be shut down, changed, or rate-limited after launch.
examples: Find My Parking Cops snapshot after SFMTA cutoff
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Add snapshot mode, source-down copy, and a postmortem path before launch.

phase_2_idea_engine.name: Use humor as legibility
phase_2_idea_engine.trigger: A technical system can be understood faster through a precise joke, pun, or reference.
phase_2_idea_engine.agent_use: Use the joke to reveal the system, not to obscure risk or imply deception.

required_safety_defaults: Avoid non-consensual biometric or attractiveness scoring
Include uncertainty notes for inferred data
Do not provide harmful scraping or evasion instructions
